rxn_class_summary: Electrocatalytic cascade assembly of benzylidenebarbiturates and cyclohexane-1,3-diones in MeOH with NaBr mediator to give dihydro-spiro[1-benzofuran-2,5'-pyrimidines]
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of MeOH generates methoxide (base) and H2; anodic oxidation of Br- generates Br2 which acts as the in-solution oxidant/halogenating mediator

key_intermediates.name: Michael adduct enolate anion B from addition of A to benzylidenebarbiturate
alpha-Bromo Michael adduct (after bromination of B by Br2)
Enolate anion C (after second deprotonation by methoxide)
Spirobarbituric dihydrofuran 3 (final product via intramolecular O-alkylation/SN2 displacement of Br)

electron_flow: Cathode reduces MeOH to MeO- (and H2), which deprotonates the 1,3-dione to anion A; A undergoes Michael addition to benzylidenebarbiturate 1 giving enolate B; anodically generated Br2 brominates B at the barbiturate alpha-carbon; a second methoxide deprotonation gives enolate C, whose dione oxygen displaces bromide intramolecularly to forge the dihydrofuran spirocycle, regenerating Br-.
notes: Mechanism proposed without explicit mechanistic probes (no TEMPO, KIE, or CV reported in shown pages); structures of products confirmed by 1H-13C HMBC. Br- functions as a redox mediator and methoxide as an in situ generated base; both base and halogen are present only in catalytic concentrations (2.4 F/mol passed).
